title: Pythagorean Theorem
summary: For a right triangle in Euclidean geometry, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides.
claim: For a right triangle on a flat Euclidean plane: a² + b² = c².

proof_layers.summary: For a right triangle in Euclidean geometry, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides.
proof_layers.intuitive_explanation: A standard area proof compares a square of side a + b containing four right triangles with the central square of side c. Subtracting the triangle areas gives a² + b² = c².
proof_layers.formal_or_structural_proof: A standard area proof compares a square of side a + b containing four right triangles with the central square of side c. Subtracting the triangle areas gives a² + b² = c².
proof_layers.empirical_or_reconstruction_basis: Reconstructable with simple objects, marks, measurement, or geometric construction depending on the truth.
proof_layers.validation_assessment: Level 5 — Mathematical / Logical Proof. Valid within Euclidean geometry for right triangles.
